items.question_text: Under 1-1-1, what are controllers required to do if they encounter situations not covered by this order?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-1, controllers must exercise their best judgment when encountering situations not covered by the order.

choices.text: Rely on phraseology from the previous canceled edition of the order.
Apply any procedure published in a foreign ATC manual that addresses the situation.
Exercise their best judgment.
Refuse to provide service until a supervisor drafts a local supplement.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-1, which provisions of this order are controllers required to be familiar with?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-1, controllers are required to be familiar with the provisions that pertain to their operational responsibilities.

choices.text: Every provision of every chapter regardless of relevance to their position.
Only the phraseology sections, because procedures are managed by supervisors.
Those that pertain to their operational responsibilities.
Only provisions that mention their specific facility by name.

items.question_text: Complete: This order prescribes air traffic control _____ and phraseology for use by persons providing air traffic control services.
items.explanation: The order prescribes both procedures and phraseology. Knowing it prescribes both (not just one) is foundational to understanding the order scope.

choices.text: procedures

items.question_text: Under 1-1-4, when JO 7110.65BB was issued, what happened to JO 7110.65AA changes?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-4, JO 7110.65AA and all changes to it are canceled.

choices.text: They remain in effect independently of the canceled basic order.
They were canceled along with the prior basic order.
They are incorporated automatically into JO 7110.65BB without review.
Only Change 1 to JO 7110.65AA was canceled; later changes survive.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-5, for how long is it advisable to retain the Explanation of Changes pages?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-5, it is advisable to retain the Explanation of Changes page or pages throughout the duration of the basic order.

choices.text: Only until each controller has read them once.
Only until the next change publication.
Throughout the duration of the basic order.
They should be discarded immediately after publication.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-9a2, what two-outcome determination must the Service Area Director make for an AJT field interpretation request?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-9a2, the Service Area Director must determine if an interpretation or a clarification is required.

choices.text: Whether the order paragraph has been superseded by a waiver.
Whether the request should be returned to the controller or kept at the service area level.
Whether the request originates from an FAA or a military facility.
Whether an interpretation or a clarification is required.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-9, how does the routing for DoD personnel requests differ from the routing for non-FAA/DoD requests?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-9d and 1-1-9e, DoD personnel must route through the chain of command to Military Service Headquarters; non-FAA/DoD may go directly to AJV-P.

choices.text: Both may be submitted directly to AJV-P without any intermediate review.
DoD requests may go directly to AJV-P; non-FAA/DoD must route through the chain of command.
Both DoD and non-FAA/DoD requests must go through the chain of command.
DoD requests must route through the chain of command to the Military Service Headquarters first; non-FAA/DoD requests may go directly to AJV-P.

items.question_text: Under the Terminal note in 1-1-11, who approves USAF procedures or minima differing from the order and involving military aircraft only?
items.explanation: The 1-1-11 Terminal note assigns this authority to Headquarters Air Force Flight Standards Agency.

choices.text: The local facility Air Force liaison officer.
Headquarters Air Force Flight Standards Agency.
AJV-P acting as the sole authority for all military-related procedures.
The Department of Defense Inspector General office.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-12, which of the following is NOT listed as a way to obtain additional ATO SMS information?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-12, SMS info channels are: SMS Toolbox, email AJI, or contact Quality Control Group. Filing a UCR is not among them.

choices.text: Emailing AJI at 9-AJI-SMS@faa.gov.
Visiting the SMS Toolbox.
Filing a UCR through the facility quality assurance program.
Contacting the service center Quality Control Group.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-12, what are all ATO employees expected to do beyond simply maintaining safety in the NAS?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-12, all ATO employees must strive to continuously improve the ATO SMS.

choices.text: Submit one SMS suggestion per calendar year at minimum.
Continuously improve the ATO SMS.
Complete SMS training only if designated as an SMS practitioner.
Report safety data only to their immediate supervisor.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-13, when contacting a non-ATO FAA regional office, which region determines the correct contact?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-13, contact the FAA region where the facility is physically located.

choices.text: The region where the facility service area office is located.
The region where the facility is physically located.
The region covering the airspace where most traffic operates.
The region designated by the facility three-letter identifier.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-14a, which of the following is listed as a recipient of this order distribution?
items.explanation: Per 1-1-14a, the Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center is among the distribution recipients.

choices.text: Foreign civil aviation authorities.
The Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center.
Every FAA regional counsel office.
All airport sponsors under an FAA grant agreement.

items.situation: A controller encounters an unusual traffic scenario not described anywhere in FAA Order JO 7110.65. A co-worker suggests the controller must stop providing service entirely until a supervisor writes a local procedure.
items.question_text: What does 1-1-1 require the controller to do when a situation is not covered by the order?

choices.text: Exercise best judgment to handle the situation while continuing to provide service.
Refuse to provide any further service until a new procedure is drafted and approved.
Apply whichever procedure feels most similar, even if it contradicts the order.
Transfer all affected aircraft to another frequency immediately.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-1, controllers exercise their best judgment when they encounter situations not covered by the order.

items.question_text: What two things does FAA Order JO 7110.65 prescribe for persons providing ATC services?

choices.text: Air traffic control procedures and phraseology.
Air traffic control procedures and staffing levels.
Only phraseology; procedures are prescribed in JO 7210.3.
Staffing levels and equipment maintenance schedules.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-1, the order prescribes both air traffic control procedures and phraseology.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-9, what happens if the Service Area Director determines more than one interpretation can be inferred?

choices.text: The request for interpretation must be submitted in writing to the Director, Policy (AJV-P).
The request is returned to the originator.
The Service Area Director issues the interpretation directly.
The request is forwarded to the FAA Office of General Counsel.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-9a2(a), if more than one interpretation can be inferred, the request must go in writing to AJV-P.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-9, how do AJR personnel requests differ from AJT field personnel requests in routing?

choices.text: AJR requests go through the Systems Operations Services Director; AJT goes through DGM and OSG.
Both follow the same path through DGM and OSG.
AJR requests skip all intermediate levels and go directly to AJV-P.
AJR requests are handled exclusively by the nearest service area office.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-9b, AJR requests follow a separate routing path through the Systems Operations Services Director.

lookup_context: Paragraph 1-1-10 references LOA development, review, and approval procedures
items.task: Identify which FAA order covers LOA development, review, and approval.
items.question_text: To find procedures for developing, reviewing, and approving a letter of agreement, which order and section does 1-1-10 point to?

choices.text: FAA Order JO 7210.3, Chapter 4, Section 3.
FAA Order JO 7110.65, Chapter 2, Section 1.
FAA Order JO 1000.37, ATO Safety Management System.
FAA Order JO 7000.5, Procedures for Submitting Changes.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-10, procedures for LOA development, review, and approval are covered in FAA Order JO 7210.3, Chapter 4, Section 3.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-11, where are the procedures to obtain approval of a waiver to air traffic procedures covered?

choices.text: FAA Order JO 7210.3, Chapter 19, Section 7.
FAA Order JO 7110.65, Chapter 2, Section 1.
FAA Order JO 1000.37, ATO Safety Management System.
FAA Order JO 7000.5, Procedures for Submitting Changes.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-11, the approval of waivers is covered in FAA Order JO 7210.3, Chapter 19, Section 7.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-12, where can all ATO employees access SMS training?

choices.text: Via eLMS.
Only at the Mike Monroney Aeronautical Center in person.
Only through the service center Quality Control Group.
SMS training is only for designated SMS practitioners.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-12, SMS training is available for all employees via eLMS.

items.question_text: Under 1-1-14b, how are subscriptions to air traffic directives made?

choices.text: Through the Air Traffic Plans and Publications website or via the GovDelivery link.
By submitting a paper form to the service area office distribution officer.
Subscriptions are automatic for all FAA employees.
By requesting a subscription through the facility IT help desk.

items.explanation: Per 1-1-14b, subscriptions are made through the FAA website or via GovDelivery.
